name: Human Readable Industry Name
description: Brief description of what this industry covers and its source validation requirements

strategy_descriptions.peer_reviewed_academic: Peer-reviewed journal articles and academic papers from established publishers
strategy_descriptions.professional_associations: Publications and guidelines from professional organizations and certification bodies
strategy_descriptions.industry_reports: Industry trend reports, market analysis, and best practice guides
strategy_descriptions.government_databases: Government publications, databases, and regulatory resources
strategy_descriptions.textbooks_continuing_education: Textbooks, manuals, and certified continuing education materials
strategy_descriptions.grey_literature_whitepapers: White papers, technical reports, and organizational publications not in mainstream databases

claim_types.statistics: Numeric claims, percentages, or measured outcomes
claim_types.regulatory: Rules, compliance requirements, or legal standards
claim_types.comparison: Comparative claims between methods, products, or approaches

replacement_source_strategies.notes: Prefer primary institutional sources before secondary commentary

authority_descriptions.AUTHORITY_TYPE: What this authority type means for this industry

claim_repositioning.description: How to reframe research-backed claims as expert-backed claims

replacements.context: For claims based on studies or data
For claims citing organizations we can't verify

claim_repositioning.authority_phrase: Based on [X] years of professional experience
authority_phrase_examples: Drawing on our extensive client work

notes: Customize each field for your specific industry. The retry_strategies array defines the order of search attempts when initial sources fail. The allowed_claims and disallowed_claims help Stage 2 understand what can be said under expert authority vs. what requires external sources.
